name: MikroTik hAP lite
title: MikroTik hAP lite
title_extended: MikroTik hAP lite - QCA9533-BL3A CPU, 32MB RAM, 4x LAN ports, 802.11b/g/n, RouterOS L4
short_description: QCA9533-BL3A CPU, 32MB RAM, 4x LAN ports, 802.11b/g/n, RouterOS L4
description: The MikroTik hAP lite is a compact and efficient home access point designed to cater to the networking needs of apartments, houses, and small offices. Despite its small size, this device is packed with features that make it a versatile and reliable networking solution. Running on MikroTik's robust RouterOS, the hAP lite provides comprehensive functionality, including bandwidth shaping, firewall, user access control, and much more. RouterOS is the operating system developed by MikroTik for their networking devices, including routers, access points, and switches. It is known for its rich feature set and flexibility, making it suitable for both simple and complex networking tasks.

detailed_description.heading: Compact Home AP for Efficient Networking
detailed_description.text: The MikroTik hAP lite is a compact and efficient home access point designed to cater to the networking needs of apartments, houses, and small offices. Despite its small size, this device is packed with features that make it a versatile and reliable networking solution. Running on MikroTik's robust RouterOS, the hAP lite provides comprehensive functionality, including bandwidth shaping, firewall, user access control, and much more. RouterOS is the operating system developed by MikroTik for their networking devices, including routers, access points, and switches. It is known for its rich feature set and flexibility, making it suitable for both simple and complex networking tasks.

detailed_description.heading: Firewall Security Features
detailed_description.text: The device supports bandwidth shaping, a technique used to control the flow of data on a network to ensure optimal performance and prevent congestion. RouterOS allows administrators to prioritize traffic, allocate bandwidth to different users or applications, and limit the maximum data rate for certain types of traffic. This ensures that critical applications receive the necessary bandwidth while preventing any single user or service from monopolizing the network. The firewall functionality in RouterOS provides robust security for the network. It can be used to create rules that allow or block traffic based on various criteria such as IP addresses, ports, and protocols. This helps protect the network from unauthorized access, malware, and other security threats. The firewall can also perform network address translation (NAT), which is essential for managing traffic between internal and external networks. User access control in RouterOS enables administrators to manage who can access the network and what resources they can use. This includes creating user profiles, setting permissions, and implementing authentication methods. By controlling user access, administrators can ensure that only authorized individuals can connect to the network and access sensitive information or services.

detailed_description.heading: Robust Performance
detailed_description.text: Powered by a 650MHz CPU integrated into the QCA9533 chipset, the MikroTik hAP lite offers a balanced mix of performance and efficiency. This CPU is adept at managing typical home or small office networking tasks, ensuring that the device can handle multiple simultaneous connections and data streams effectively. The QCA9533 chipset, designed by Qualcomm Atheros, provides advanced networking capabilities, supporting various network standards and contributing to the device’s overall reliability and performance. Equipped with 32MB of RAM, the hAP lite is designed to efficiently run RouterOS and manage network traffic. This memory capacity is sufficient for everyday networking tasks, allowing the device to handle multiple user connections, run additional services like DHCP and DNS, and manage firewall rules without significant lag. The 32MB RAM ensures smooth operation even under moderate network loads, making it suitable for home and small office environments. The hAP lite features dual-chain 2.4GHz onboard wireless, supporting the 802.11b/g/n standards. The dual-chain system, which uses two spatial streams, enhances the device's ability to provide stable and robust wireless connections. This setup reduces interference, increases coverage area, and improves overall network performance. The hAP lite offers a maximum radio rate of up to 300Mbps on the 2.4GHz band, achievable using 802.11n technology with dual-chain MIMO. This radio rate ensures sufficient speed for multiple users to engage in activities like streaming HD videos, video conferencing, and online gaming simultaneously without significant lag or interruptions. In addition to its robust wireless capabilities, the hAP lite features four Fast Ethernet ports, each supporting data transfer rates up to 100 Mbps. These wired connections are crucial for devices that require stable and high-speed internet access, such as desktop computers, smart TVs, and gaming consoles. Wired connections typically offer more stability and are less prone to interference compared to wireless connections, ensuring consistent performance for critical devices. The 100 Mbps Ethernet ports are also suitable for connecting essential network infrastructure components like switches and routers, providing a reliable network backbone.

detailed_description.heading: Efficient Design & Easy Management
detailed_description.text: The MikroTik hAP lite is designed with user convenience at its core. It is powered via a widely used MicroUSB connector, offering a reliable and flexible power solution. This standard allows the device to be powered using readily available MicroUSB adapters or even USB ports on computers and other electronics, eliminating the need for specialized power supplies. With a maximum power consumption of just 3.5 W, the hAP lite is an energy-efficient choice that won't significantly impact your power bills. Additionally, the ability to easily replace the power adapter enhances the device's usability and convenience. Engineered to perform reliably in diverse environmental conditions, the hAP lite boasts an impressive operating temperature range of -20 °C to 70 °C. This broad temperature tolerance ensures that the device can function effectively in a variety of settings, from cold outdoor installations to hot indoor environments. Such versatility is particularly beneficial for users in regions with extreme weather conditions, providing consistent performance and durability across different scenarios. The hAP lite is suitable for both indoor and outdoor applications, provided it is housed appropriately. Preconfigured for ease of use, the hAP lite allows for quick and hassle-free setup. Simply connect the power and your internet cable, and you're ready to start using your network. This plug-and-play approach significantly simplifies the installation process, making it ideal for users who prefer minimal setup hassle. The hAP lite comes equipped with a RouterOS L4 license, unlocking a wide range of advanced features and capabilities within MikroTik's powerful and flexible operating system. The L4 license includes comprehensive functionalities suitable for both home and small business networks, such as bandwidth shaping, firewall capabilities, user access control, VPN support, and more. This extensive feature set allows users to customize and optimize their network setup according to their specific needs, ensuring a robust and secure networking solution.
